chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Fasting Obligations for the mutamatti' Who Cannot Afford a Sacrificial Animal

text: 3097 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ مَاتَ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ هَدْيٌ لِمُتْعَتِهِ فَلْيَصُمْ عَنْهُ وَلِيُّهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3097 - Safwan narrated from Muawiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a person dies and does not have a hady (sacrificial animal) for his Tamattu', his guardian should fast on his behalf."
